name: Jaime the Bearded Trail
name_original: Sendero de Jaime el Barbudo (PR-CV 255) — circular desde Hondón de los Frailes
description: Hike the Sendero de Jaime el Barbudo (PR-CV 255), a moderate 7.4-mile circular trail from Hondón de los Frailes with historical caves and vi
region_intro: Hiking around Hondón De Los Frailes offers diverse natural landscapes, characterized by mountainous terrain, rich agricultural land, and unique geological formations. The region is situated at the foot of the Sierra de los Frailes, providing numerous trails into surrounding hills and mountains. Hikers can traverse paths winding through Mediterranean vegetation, including thyme, rosemary, lavender, and esparto grasses, alongside vineyards, almond trees, and olive groves. Notable geological features include the Rio Chicamo, with its prehistoric landscape, and La Rambla Salada, a natural space featuring a channel eroded by meteorological agents. Best hiking trails around Hondón De Los Frailes The most popular hiking route is Cueva Jaime el Barbudo, subida al Picacho — circular desde el Puerto de Albatera, a 4.6 miles (7.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 47 minutes to complete. This route requires good fitness and offers mostly accessible paths. Another top favourite among local hikers is Jaime the Bearded Trail, a moderate 7.4 miles (11.8 km) path. This trail combines nature, history, and views, leading hikers through historical caves and past the viewpoint of Jaime el Barbudo. Local hikers also love the Hondón de los Frailes – View of El Cantón loop from Hondón de los Frailes, a 6.1 miles (9.8 km) trail leading through varied terrain with views towards El Cantón, often completed in about 2 hours 38 minutes. Hiking around Hondón De Los Frailes is defined by mountainous terrain, Mediterranean landscapes, and unique geological formations like the Rio Chicamo and La Rambla Salada. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more challenging ascents. The routes in Hondón De Los Frailes are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 400 reviews. More than 2000 hikers have used komoot to explore Hondón De Los Frailes's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Hondón De Los Frailes?
A: Hondón De Los Frailes offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 100 trails documented on komoot. These range from easy strolls to more challenging ascents, catering to different fitness levels.

Q: What types of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in this region?
A: The region around Hondón De Los Frailes boasts diverse natural landscapes. You'll find mountainous terrain, rich agricultural land with vineyards, almond, and olive groves, and unique geological formations. Trails often wind through Mediterranean vegetation like thyme, rosemary, and lavender.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly hiking options?
A: Yes, Hondón De Los Frailes has options for all skill levels. Approximately 15 of the trails are rated as easy, making them suitable for families or those looking for a more relaxed walk. Many moderate trails also feature accessible paths.

Q: What are some unique natural features or attractions to look out for?
A: Hikers can explore several unique natural features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4852429" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chicamo River Canyon&lt;/a&gt; presents a prehistoric landscape with outstanding geology. Another notable spot is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6136157" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Rambla Salada&lt;/a&gt;, a spectacular natural space with a distinctive channel eroded by meteorological agents and a small saltwater spring. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4615694" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cave of Jaime el Barbudo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Hondón De Los Frailes?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. Popular circular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1316891178/cueva-jaime-el-barbudo-subida-al-picacho-circular-desde-el-puerto-de-albatera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva Jaime el Barbudo, subida al Picacho&lt;/a&gt; route and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17270878" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hondón de los Frailes – View of El Cantón loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of views can I expect from the trails?
A: The mountainous terrain, particularly around the Sierra de los Frailes, offers magnificent panoramic views. From peaks like Picacho or viewpoints along the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1316815985/jaime-the-bearded-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jaime the Bearded Trail&lt;/a&gt;, you can often see the Sierra de Crevillente, the mountains of Callosa and Orihuela, El Hondo Natural Park, and on clear days, even the Torrevieja salt lakes and the Mediterranean Sea.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Hondón De Los Frailes?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 400 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse natural landscapes, the combination of nature and history, and the stunning panoramic views from various viewpoints.

Q: How long do hikes typically take in this area?
A: Hiking durations vary depending on the route and your pace. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1316891178/cueva-jaime-el-barbudo-subida-al-picacho-circular-desde-el-puerto-de-albatera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva Jaime el Barbudo, subida al Picacho&lt;/a&gt; trail takes around 2 hours 47 minutes, while the longer &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1316815985/jaime-the-bearded-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jaime the Bearded Trail&lt;/a&gt; typically takes about 3 hours 28 minutes.

Q: Are there any trails that feature waterfalls?
A: Yes, you can find waterfalls in the region. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4444388" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Final Waterfall of the Via Ferrata&lt;/a&gt; is one such highlight that hikers can discover.

Q: Is Hondón De Los Frailes accessible by public transport for hikers?
A: While the town of Hondón De Los Frailes is generally accessible, specific public transport links directly to trailheads can be limited. It's advisable to check local bus schedules or consider driving to reach the starting points of many hiking routes.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Hondón De Los Frailes?
A: Due to its Mediterranean climate, Hondón De Los Frailes offers good hiking conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are particularly pleasant, with milder temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended, while winters are generally mild.

Q: Are there any trails that lead to interesting rock formations?
A: Absolutely. Besides the unique geology of the Rio Chicamo and La Rambla Salada, you can also explore areas featuring specific rock formations like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5999594" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Ventanas Rock Formation&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5999625" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Barrón Negro&lt;/a&gt;, which add to the scenic diversity of the hikes.
tips: Hondón de los Frailes is a municipality in the Medio Vinalopó region, near Murcia. Located at the foot of the Sierra de los Frailes, the town offers several hiking trails to various hills, caves and vineyards.

Hondón de los Frailes viewpoint with a beautiful information panel made of tiles. Views of the El Alto del Rincón de los Tiesos peak, Crevillente and coast.

Jaime El Barbudo was a Spanish bandit from the early 19th century.

A narrow but very high crevice leading to the cave. It is more than 20 meters long and you have to be careful and especially with light, as it is very dark inside. The gorge ends in an area of steps that descend very steeply and end at the entrance to the pit.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Sendero de Jaime el Barbudo (PR-CV 255) hike?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 11.8 kilometers (7.3 miles) long and generally takes about 3 hours and 27 minutes to complete. This duration can vary depending on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: Is the trail suitable for beginners or families with children?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Sendero de Jaime el Barbudo (PR-CV 255) is generally considered to have a moderate difficulty, making it accessible to hikers with a reasonable level of fitness. Some sections are even rated as easy, making it suitable for families and children. However, sturdy hiking boots are recommended due to varied and sometimes uneven terrain.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail starts and ends in Hondón de los Frailes, where parking is readily available. You can easily find a spot in the village before beginning your hike.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers several notable sights. You'll pass through the charming village of Hondón de los Frailes, reach the Jaime el Barbudo Viewpoint for panoramic vistas, and discover the historic Cave of Jaime el Barbudo, a former hideout of the legendary bandit.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: The route features varied terrain, including natural paths and sections that wind through traditional almond and olive groves. While there's a gradual climb to the highest point, the path is generally manageable. Sturdy hiking boots are advisable for comfort and stability on uneven sections.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The Mediterranean climate makes this trail enjoyable for much of the year. Spring and autumn are particularly pleasant, with milder temperatures and blooming flora. Even in warmer months, an early start can make the hike more comfortable. Always check the weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Is the Sendero de Jaime el Barbudo (PR-CV 255) dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through agricultural areas or near wildlife, and to carry enough water for both yourself and your pet.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to hike the Sendero de Jaime el Barbudo (PR-CV 255). It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What should I bring for the hike?
faqs.answer: It's essential to bring adequate water, especially during warmer months. Sturdy hiking boots are recommended for the varied terrain. Sun protection (hat, sunscreen) is also advisable. Depending on the season, layers of clothing can be useful.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and wildlife can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: You'll enjoy panoramic views of the surrounding mountains and valleys, with the landscape changing colors throughout the seasons. The trail passes through traditional agricultural areas with almond and olive groves. You might also encounter hardy Mediterranean herbs like rosemary and thyme. Birdwatchers could spot various species, including the Bonelli's eagle and bee-eater.

faqs.question: Are there any historical or cultural highlights on the route?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. The trail is named after Jaime "El Barbudo" (Jaime the Bearded), a legendary 19th-century bandit, and leads to one of his hideouts, the Cave of Jaime el Barbudo (sometimes referred to as "Cueva de la Excomunión"). You'll also pass through traditional "fincas" (rural estates), offering a glimpse into the region's rural heritage.
